steps.title: Track the tempting view
steps.content: The underlined portion first presents a possible interpretation: fast painting accounts for the Highwaymen's ethereal qualities. It then rejects that interpretation as inaccurate. The reason is that Hair's method was not universally practiced by his affiliates, and Buckner achieved the same effects while painting with greater deliberateness.

steps.title: Reject invented movement claims
steps.content: Choice A invents a long-standing disagreement within the group. Choice C invents a central-versus-peripheral contrast, but the text does not frame Buckner that way. Choice D reverses the origin of the technique, since Hair pioneered fast painting and others adopted it. The underlined portion considers and rebuts an interpretation about a technique's effect on the group's aesthetic. The answer is B.
